tutorials.title: Kolmogorov's Axioms of Probability
tutorials.intro: The three axioms that define a probability measure and the probability-space triple
tutorials.example: Construct an explicit probability space for a fair die
tutorials.r_demo: Simulate rolls; verify empirical frequencies approach axiomatic probabilities
pitfalls: Confusing axiomatic probability with long-run frequency

tutorials.title: Sample Space and Events
tutorials.intro: Sample space as the set of all possible outcomes; events as subsets and their set operations
theory: Union A U B, intersection A ∩ B, complement A^c
tutorials.example: Two dice: enumerate the 36 outcomes and compute P(sum = 7)
tutorials.r_demo: Use expand.grid to enumerate the sample space

tutorials.title: Conditional Probability
tutorials.intro: Probability of A given B; the product rule and its uses
tutorials.example: Screening test: probability of disease given positive result
tutorials.r_demo: Build a 2x2 population table and compute all conditional probabilities

tutorials.title: Independence
tutorials.intro: Two events are independent when information about one does not change the probability of the other
tutorials.example: Two coin tosses; verify independence numerically
tutorials.r_demo: Simulate paired Bernoulli trials and verify factorisation empirically

tutorials.title: Bayes' Theorem
tutorials.intro: Inverting conditional probabilities: converting P(B|A) into P(A|B)
theory: Prior, likelihood, posterior; the Bayesian update rule
tutorials.example: Diagnostic test with known sensitivity and specificity, varying prevalence
tutorials.r_demo: Compute posterior probability of disease across a grid of prevalences
pitfalls: Confusing sensitivity with positive predictive value

tutorials.title: Law of Total Probability
tutorials.intro: Decomposing a marginal probability over a partition of the sample space
theory: P(A) = sum_i P(A|B_i)P(B_i) for a partition {B_i}
tutorials.example: Multi-stage clinical pathway; probability of referral as sum over stages
tutorials.r_demo: Compute P(event) by conditioning on a latent group variable

tutorials.title: Random Variables
tutorials.intro: A random variable as a measurable function from sample space to real numbers
theory: Pre-image of Borel sets has probability
tutorials.example: Coin flip mapped to {0, 1}; die roll as a random variable
tutorials.r_demo: Simulate and summarise distributions of simple random variables
pitfalls: Confusing the random variable with its realisation

tutorials.title: Discrete vs Continuous Variables
tutorials.intro: Countable vs uncountable support, with mixed distributions as a subtle middle ground
theory: PMF for discrete, PDF for continuous
tutorials.example: Number of events (discrete) vs waiting time (continuous)
tutorials.r_demo: Plot PMF of Poisson vs PDF of exponential
pitfalls: Treating continuous as discrete or vice versa
Ignoring mixed distributions in censored data

tutorials.title: The Probability Mass Function
tutorials.intro: PMF as the probability of each value in a discrete distribution
theory: p(x) = P(X = x); sum over support equals 1
tutorials.example: Binomial and Poisson PMFs visualised
tutorials.r_demo: Use dbinom and dpois to compute PMFs and plot
pitfalls: Forgetting to normalise when defining a custom PMF

tutorials.title: The Probability Density Function
tutorials.intro: PDF integrates to probability; density at a point is not probability
theory: P(a < X < b) = integral of f from a to b
Density can exceed 1 at any point
tutorials.example: Normal PDF; area under the curve for an interval
tutorials.r_demo: Use dnorm and integrate to recover probabilities
pitfalls: Forgetting to integrate when computing interval probabilities

tutorials.title: The Cumulative Distribution Function
tutorials.intro: CDF as F(x) = P(X <= x); unifying discrete and continuous distributions
tutorials.example: ECDF of sample data vs theoretical CDF
tutorials.r_demo: Use pnorm, pbinom and plot ECDF with ecdf()

tutorials.title: Expectation of a Random Variable
tutorials.intro: The expected value as the probability-weighted average of outcomes
theory: Discrete: E[X] = sum x p(x); continuous: E[X] = integral x f(x) dx
LOTUS: E[g(X)] without finding distribution of g(X)
tutorials.example: Expected value of a sum of die rolls
tutorials.r_demo: Monte Carlo estimate of E[X] and E[g(X)]

tutorials.title: Variance and Covariance
tutorials.intro: Second moments: spread of a single variable and joint variation of two
tutorials.example: Bivariate normal; compute theoretical vs sample covariance
tutorials.r_demo: rmvnorm from mvtnorm; compare theoretical Sigma to sample covariance
pitfalls: Zero covariance does not imply independence
Sample variance uses n-1, not n

tutorials.title: The Correlation Coefficient
tutorials.intro: Standardised covariance bounded in [-1, 1]; measures linear association
tutorials.example: Simulate bivariate normal at several rho levels
tutorials.r_demo: cor() on simulated data; visualise scatter

tutorials.title: Joint Distributions
tutorials.intro: Joint PMF/PDF of two random variables and how they encode dependence
tutorials.example: Bivariate normal with known correlation
tutorials.r_demo: Visualise 2D density via geom_density_2d

tutorials.title: Marginal Distributions
tutorials.intro: Integrating out one variable in a joint distribution to get the marginal of the other
theory: Marginals do not determine the joint
Marginal of a bivariate normal is normal
tutorials.example: Marginalise out site in a multicentre bivariate normal model
tutorials.r_demo: Derive marginal from simulated joint sample
pitfalls: Missing probability mass by improper integration

tutorials.title: Conditional Distributions
tutorials.intro: Distribution of X given Y = y; conditional expectation as a function of y
tutorials.example: Bivariate normal: conditional distribution is normal
tutorials.r_demo: Compute conditional mean/variance analytically for MVN
pitfalls: Confusing E[X|Y] (a function) with E[X|Y=y] (a number)

tutorials.title: The Bernoulli Distribution
tutorials.intro: Single trial with success probability p
theory: MLE of p is the sample proportion
tutorials.example: Successful drug response in a single patient
tutorials.r_demo: rbinom(n, 1, p); empirical proportion
pitfalls: Treating a sum of Bernoullis as Bernoulli

tutorials.title: The Binomial Distribution
tutorials.intro: Sum of n independent Bernoulli trials with fixed probability p
tutorials.example: Number of responders out of 30 patients
tutorials.r_demo: dbinom for exact, normal approximation, simulation verification
pitfalls: Using normal approximation with small np or n(1-p)

tutorials.title: The Poisson Distribution
tutorials.intro: Count of rare events per unit time/space with rate lambda
tutorials.example: Number of emergency admissions per hour
tutorials.r_demo: dpois, rpois, plot histogram vs PMF
pitfalls: Overdispersion where Var > mean: use negative binomial

tutorials.title: The Geometric Distribution
tutorials.intro: Trials to the first success in independent Bernoulli attempts
tutorials.example: Number of IVF cycles to conception
tutorials.r_demo: rgeom, dgeom; demonstrate memoryless property via conditional probability
pitfalls: Different R convention for counting trials (failures vs total)

tutorials.title: The Negative Binomial Distribution
tutorials.intro: Overdispersed counts; sum of geometric distributions or Gamma-Poisson mixture
theory: PMF parameterised by size and prob or mu and dispersion
Used for overdispersed RNA-seq and count data
tutorials.example: Hospital readmissions with between-patient heterogeneity
tutorials.r_demo: rnbinom; fit via MASS::glm.nb

tutorials.title: The Hypergeometric Distribution
tutorials.intro: Sampling without replacement from a finite population
tutorials.example: Sampling for a quality-audit inspection
tutorials.r_demo: dhyper; compare to binomial approximation as N grows
pitfalls: Treating as binomial when the population is small

tutorials.title: The Multinomial Distribution
tutorials.intro: k-category generalisation of the binomial with probabilities summing to one
theory: Marginals are binomial; pairs are negatively correlated
tutorials.example: ABO blood-group counts in a donor pool
tutorials.r_demo: rmultinom; chi-squared goodness-of-fit for observed vs expected
pitfalls: Using chi-squared with expected counts below 5

tutorials.title: The Uniform Distribution
tutorials.intro: Equal probability over an interval (continuous) or set (discrete)
theory: Used for simulation seeds and resampling
tutorials.example: Randomisation allocation schemes
tutorials.r_demo: runif; inverse-CDF method to generate non-uniform draws

tutorials.title: The Exponential Distribution
tutorials.intro: Waiting time between Poisson events; memoryless continuous distribution
tutorials.example: Time to first device failure in a reliability study
tutorials.r_demo: rexp, pexp; hazard plot (constant for exponential)
pitfalls: Forcing exponential when hazard is non-constant

tutorials.title: The Gamma Distribution
tutorials.intro: Flexible right-skewed distribution for positive continuous data
theory: E[X] = alpha/beta; special cases exponential (alpha=1), chi-squared
tutorials.example: Dwell times in a clinic visit
tutorials.r_demo: rgamma; fit shape and rate via MASS::fitdistr
pitfalls: Shape vs scale vs rate confusion across software
Boundary: values exactly zero not allowed

tutorials.title: The Beta Distribution
tutorials.intro: Distribution over [0, 1] for probabilities and proportions
theory: E[X] = a/(a+b); conjugate prior for binomial
tutorials.example: Prior on drug-response rate in a phase II trial
tutorials.r_demo: dbeta; visualise Beta(a,b) for several parameter pairs

tutorials.title: The Chi-Squared Distribution
tutorials.intro: Sum of squared independent standard normals; degrees of freedom parameter
theory: df = k positive integer; mean k, variance 2k
tutorials.example: Distribution of sample variance in normal samples
tutorials.r_demo: rchisq; compare sum of rnorm()^2 empirically
pitfalls: Conflating df of chi-squared with sample size n
Using chi-squared tests with small expected counts

tutorials.title: Student's t-Distribution
tutorials.intro: Ratio of a standard normal to the scaled square root of an independent chi-squared
theory: Heavier tails than normal; converges to normal as df grows
Used for t-tests and small-sample CIs
tutorials.example: Small-sample CI for a mean
tutorials.r_demo: rt; overlay t-density and normal density at several df
pitfalls: Using normal approximation in small samples

tutorials.title: The F Distribution
tutorials.intro: Ratio of two independent scaled chi-squared random variables
theory: F(df1, df2); used in ANOVA and variance tests
Reciprocal F also follows F distribution
tutorials.example: F-statistic in one-way ANOVA
tutorials.r_demo: df; qf for critical values; pf for p-values
pitfalls: Forgetting numerator vs denominator df ordering

tutorials.title: The Weibull Distribution
tutorials.intro: Flexible positive-valued distribution with monotone hazard function
theory: Shape k and scale lambda parameters
Used in reliability and survival analysis
tutorials.example: Time to device failure; time to tumour recurrence
tutorials.r_demo: rweibull; hazard plot for increasing vs decreasing hazard
pitfalls: Missing the accelerated failure time interpretation

tutorials.title: The Log-Normal Distribution
tutorials.intro: Distribution of X where log(X) is normal; multiplicative processes
theory: PDF involves log(x); mean exp(mu + sigma^2/2)
tutorials.example: Serum enzyme concentrations
tutorials.r_demo: rlnorm; compare arithmetic and geometric means

tutorials.title: The Multivariate Normal Distribution
tutorials.intro: Generalisation of the normal to multiple correlated variables
tutorials.example: Bivariate normal for paired biomarkers
tutorials.r_demo: rmvnorm; visualise with 2D density; conditional distribution
pitfalls: Confusing correlation matrix with covariance matrix

tutorials.title: Copulas: An Introduction
tutorials.intro: Separating marginal distributions from the dependence structure via Sklar's theorem
tutorials.example: Joint modelling of two biomarkers with non-normal marginals
tutorials.r_demo: copula package: fit and simulate from a bivariate Gaussian copula
pitfalls: Fitting copulas without checking marginal fits
Confusing copula dependence with linear correlation

tutorials.title: Transformations of Random Variables
tutorials.intro: Distribution of g(X) from distribution of X; change of variables
tutorials.example: Square of a standard normal is chi-squared with 1 df
tutorials.r_demo: Simulate X, compute g(X), compare histogram to theoretical density

tutorials.title: Convolutions of Distributions
tutorials.intro: Distribution of the sum of two independent random variables
theory: Closure: sums of normals are normal, of Poissons are Poisson
tutorials.example: Sum of two independent Poisson event counts
tutorials.r_demo: Simulate X+Y and compare to theoretical convolved PDF
pitfalls: Convolving PMFs of variables with different supports

tutorials.title: The Hazard Function
tutorials.intro: Instantaneous rate of event given survival so far
tutorials.example: Hazard of device failure over time
tutorials.r_demo: Plot empirical hazard vs theoretical for exponential, Weibull
pitfalls: Assuming constant hazard when it is clearly time-varying

tutorials.title: The Survival Function
tutorials.intro: Probability of surviving past time t: S(t) = 1 - F(t) = P(T > t)
theory: Relation to hazard: S(t) = exp(-integral_0^t h(u) du)
tutorials.example: Visualise S(t) for an exponential vs Weibull with increasing hazard
tutorials.r_demo: survfit on simulated data; overlay theoretical survival curves
pitfalls: Reporting median survival when censoring is extreme
